author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
akrodha paramānanda nityānanda⋅rāya
abhimāna⋅śūnya nitāi nagare beḍāya

adhama patita jīvera dvāre dvāre giyā
harināma⋅mahāmantra dicchena vilāiyā

jā’re dekhe tā’re kahe dante tṛṇa dhari’
‘āmāre kiniyā laha, bala gaura⋅hari’

eta bali’ nityānanda bhūme gaḍi’ jāya
sonāra parvata jena dhūlāte loṭāya

hena avatāre jā’ra rati nā janmila
locana bale sei pāpī ela āra gela
en_translation:
Free from anger and full of supreme bliss is Lord Nityānanda; devoid of all ego, He wanders through the city.

Going from door to door to reach the most fallen souls, He is distributing the great mantra of the Holy Name.

To whomever He sees He says, holding a straw in His teeth: "Please purchase Me; just say Gaura⋅hari!"

Saying this, Nityānanda rolls on the ground, like a golden mountain rolling in the dust.

For one in whom attraction was not born for such an incarnation, Locana says: that sinner has simply wasted his life.
